# Nested log normalization (lowercase/replex/replace) then regex extract and wildcard filter

**Dataset**: `gx_09_logs`

Demonstrates the canonical normalize-then-extract pipeline for noisy free-text logs. A single `alter` nests three string transforms inside-out: `lowercase(line)` case-folds, `replex(..., "\s+", " ")` collapses runs of whitespace via regex, and `replace(..., "  ", " ")` cleans any remaining double space — yielding a stable `clean` field that downstream extraction can rely on. `regextract` then pulls the status code from the cleaned text, `string_count(clean, "status")` confirms the token is present, and the `filter` keeps only lines whose status matches the `?0?` wildcard (e.g. 200/404/500-style middle-zero codes). Gotcha: string functions compose right-to-left when nested, so build the cleanup chain from the innermost transform outward.

```sql
dataset = gx_09_logs
| alter clean = replace(replex(lowercase(line), "\s+", " "), "  ", " ")
| alter status = arrayindex(regextract(clean, "status=(\d{3})"), 0)
| alter n_status_tok = string_count(clean, "status")
| filter n_status_tok >= 1 and wildcard_match(status, "?0?")
| fields clean, status, n_status_tok
| sort desc status
```
